controls.name: Managed Device for Security Info Registration
Global Admins as Local Admin on Join
Local Admin Assignment on Entra Join
Third-Party Storage in M365 Web Apps
SSPR Registration Campaign Targets All Users
External Users Can Respond to Forms

ENTRA-APPS-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Users > User settings > Users can register applications > No. Also review Enterprise applications > User settings > Users can consent to apps.

ENTRA-CONSENT-001.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -DefaultUserRolePermissions @{PermissionGrantPoliciesAssigned = @()}. Entra admin center > Enterprise applications > Consent and permissions.

ENTRA-APPREG-001.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -DefaultUserRolePermissions @{AllowedToCreateApps = $false}. Entra admin center > Users > User settings.

ENTRA-AUTHMETHOD-002.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Email OTP > Disable. Email OTP is a weaker authentication factor.

ENTRA-AUTHMETHOD-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > SMS > Disable. SMS is vulnerable to SIM-swapping attacks.

ENTRA-AUTHMETHOD-004.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Settings > System-preferred multifactor authentication > Enabled.

ENTRA-DEVICE-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Users may join devices to Microsoft Entra > Selected. Restrict to a specific group of authorized users.

ENTRA-DEVICE-003.1.setting: Global Admins as Local Admin on Join
ENTRA-DEVICE-003.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Global administrator is added as local administrator on the device during Azure AD Join > No.

ENTRA-DEVICE-002.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Maximum number of devices per user. Set to 15 or lower.

ENTRA-LINKEDIN-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Users > User settings > LinkedIn account connections > No. Prevents data leakage between LinkedIn and organizational directory.

ENTRA-GROUP-002.1.recommended_value: At least 1 dynamic group for guests
ENTRA-GROUP-002.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Groups > New group > Membership type = Dynamic User > Rule: (user.userType -eq "Guest"). This enables targeted policies for guest users.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.14.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.12.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.11.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.13.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-SSPR-001.1.setting: SSPR Registration Campaign Targets All Users
ENTRA-SSPR-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Registration campaign > Enable and target All Users.

ENTRA-SECDEFAULT-001.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyIdentitySecurityDefaultsEnforcementPolicy -IsEnabled $true. Entra admin center > Properties > Manage security defaults.

ENTRA-ADMIN-002.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Identity > Users > User settings > Administration center > verify "Restrict access to Microsoft Entra admin center" is set to Yes.

ENTRA-ADMIN-003.1.remediation: Create 2+ cloud-only emergency access accounts with Global Administrator role, excluded from all Conditional Access policies. Use naming convention including "BreakGlass" or "Emergen...

ENTRA-PERUSER-001.1.recommended_value: All per-user MFA disabled (use CA policies)
ENTRA-PERUSER-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Users > Per-user MFA > Ensure all users show Disabled. Use Conditional Access policies for MFA enforcement instead.

ENTRA-DEVICE-006.1.current_value: Review -- verify users cannot read own BitLocker keys
ENTRA-DEVICE-006.1.recommended_value: Users restricted from recovering BitLocker keys
ENTRA-DEVICE-006.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Restrict users from recovering the BitLocker key(s) for their owned devices > Yes.

ENTRA-GUEST-002.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -AllowInvitesFrom 'adminsAndGuestInviters'. Entra admin center > External Identities > External collaboration settings.

ENTRA-ORGSETTING-002.1.remediation: M365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Microsoft Forms > ensure internal phishing protection is enabled.

ENTRA-ORGSETTING-004.1.remediation: M365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Bookings > restrict shared booking pages to selected staff members.

ENTRA-ORGSETTING-003.1.setting: Third-Party Storage in M365 Web Apps
ENTRA-ORGSETTING-003.1.remediation: M365 admin center > Settings > Org settings > Microsoft 365 on the web > uncheck all third-party storage services.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-005.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Password protection. Verify on-premises password protection is enabled.

ENTRA-PIM-002.1.current_value: PIM is available but not configured in this tenant
ENTRA-PIM-002.1.recommended_value: At least 1 access review for guests
ENTRA-PIM-002.1.remediation: This check requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5). Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Access reviews.

ENTRA-PIM-003.1.current_value: PIM is available but not configured in this tenant
ENTRA-PIM-003.1.recommended_value: At least 1 access review for admin roles
ENTRA-PIM-003.1.remediation: This check requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5). Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Access reviews.

ENTRA-PIM-004.1.current_value: PIM is available but not configured in this tenant
ENTRA-PIM-004.1.remediation: This check requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5). Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Azure AD roles > Settings.

ENTRA-PIM-001.1.current_value: PIM is available but not configured in this tenant
ENTRA-PIM-001.1.recommended_value: PIM enabled for all privileged roles
ENTRA-PIM-001.1.remediation: This check requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5). Enable PIM at Entra admin center > Identity Governance > Privileged Identity Management.

ENTRA-PIM-005.1.current_value: PIM is available but not configured in this tenant
ENTRA-PIM-005.1.remediation: This check requires Entra ID P2 (included in M365 E5). Entra admin center > Identity Governance > PIM > Azure AD roles > Settings.

ENTRA-SECDEFAULT-001.2.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyIdentitySecurityDefaultsEnforcementPolicy -IsEnabled $true. Entra admin center > Properties > Manage security defaults.

ENTRA-CONSENT-002.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyAdminConsentRequestPolicy -IsEnabled $true. Entra admin center > Enterprise applications > Admin consent requests.

ENTRA-AUTHMETHOD-001.2.remediation: Entra admin center > Protection > Authentication methods > Voice call > Disable. Voice is vulnerable to telephony-based attacks.

ENTRA-CA-003.1.remediation: Run: Get-MgIdentityConditionalAccessPolicy | Where-Object {$_.State -eq 'enabled'}. Ensure policies are set to On, not Report-only.

ENTRA-DEVICE-004.1.setting: Local Admin Assignment on Entra Join
ENTRA-DEVICE-004.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Manage Additional local administrators on all Azure AD joined devices. Minimize additional local admins.

ENTRA-DEVICE-005.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Devices > Device settings > Enable Microsoft Entra Local Administrator Password Solution (LAPS) > Yes.

ENTRA-TENANT-001.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgPolicyAuthorizationPolicy -DefaultUserRolePermissions @{AllowedToCreateTenants = $false}. Entra admin center > Users > User settings.

ENTRA-GUEST-004.1.remediation: Entra admin center > External Identities > External collaboration settings > Collaboration restrictions > Allow invitations only to the specified domains.

ENTRA-ORGSETTING-001.1.remediation: Entra admin center > Enterprise applications > Consent and permissions > User consent settings > Do not allow user consent.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.3.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.7.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.6.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.10.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.1.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.9.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.4.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.5.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.8.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.15.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.16.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

ENTRA-PASSWORD-001.2.remediation: Run: Update-MgDomain -DomainId {domain} -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 2147483647. M365 admin center > Settings > Password expiration policy.

CA-DEVICECODE-001.1.remediation: Create a CA policy: Target All users > Conditions > Authentication flows > Device code flow > Grant > Block access. Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access.

CA-DEVICE-002.1.setting: Managed Device for Security Info Registration
CA-DEVICE-002.1.remediation: Create a CA policy: User actions > Register security information > Grant > Require compliant device. Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access.

CA-PHISHRES-001.1.remediation: Create a CA policy: Target admin roles > Grant > Require authentication strength > Phishing-resistant MFA. Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access.

CA-USERRISK-001.1.remediation: Create a CA policy: Target All users > Conditions > User risk > High > Grant > Require password change + MFA. Entra admin center > Protection > Conditional Access.

CA-LEGACYAUTH-001.1.current_value: Yes (2 policy: Sperren von Legacy Apps; M365 Lighthouse - Block Legacy Authentication)

CA-MFA-ADMIN-001.1.current_value: Yes (2 policy: Baseline - Require MFA for Admins; Microsoft-managed: Multifactor authentication for admins accessing Microsoft Admin Portals)

CA-SIGNINRISK-002.1.current_value: Yes (1 policy: Microsoft-managed: Multifactor authentication and reauthentication for risky sign-ins)

CA-SIGNINRISK-001.1.current_value: Yes (1 policy: Microsoft-managed: Multifactor authentication and reauthentication for risky sign-ins)

EXO-DIRECTSEND-001.1.remediation: Connect to Exchange Online PowerShell to check inbound connector configuration.

INTUNE-ENROLL-001.1.remediation: This check requires Intune license and DeviceManagementConfiguration.Read.All permission.

DEFENDER-PRIORITY-001.1.remediation: Connect to Exchange Online PowerShell to check preset security policy rules.

DEFENDER-PRIORITY-002.1.remediation: Connect to Exchange Online PowerShell to check preset security policy rules.

DEFENDER-SAFEATTACH-001.1.remediation: Safe Attachments requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 or higher.

DEFENDER-SAFEATTACH-002.1.remediation: Safe Attachments for SPO/OneDrive/Teams requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 or higher.

DEFENDER-SAFELINKS-001.1.remediation: Safe Links requires Defender for Office 365 Plan 1 or higher.

DEFENDER-ZAP-001.1.recommended_value: Defender for Office 365 with Teams ZAP
DEFENDER-ZAP-001.1.remediation: ZAP for Teams requires Defender for Office 365. Upgrade license to enable this capability.

COMPLIANCE-AUDIT-001.1.remediation: Connect to Security & Compliance PowerShell to check audit log configuration.

COMPLIANCE-DLP-001.1.remediation: Connect to Security & Compliance PowerShell to check DLP policies.

COMPLIANCE-LABELS-001.1.remediation: Connect to Security & Compliance PowerShell to check sensitivity labels.

PURVIEW-RETENTION-001.1.remediation: Connect to Security & Compliance PowerShell to check retention policies: Connect-Service -Service Purview.

TEAMS-APPS-002.1.current_value: Cannot be fully checked via API
TEAMS-APPS-002.1.recommended_value: Block third-party apps or restrict to approved list
TEAMS-APPS-002.1.remediation: Teams admin center > Teams apps > Permission policies > Org-wide app settings > Third-party apps > Off (or restrict to approved apps).

TEAMS-REPORTING-001.1.remediation: Teams admin center > Messaging policies > Global (Org-wide default) > Report a security concern > On.

FORMS-CONFIG-001.1.setting: External Users Can Respond to Forms
FORMS-CONFIG-001.1.remediation: Reconnect with the OrgSettings-Forms.Read.All permission scope to check Microsoft Forms settings.
